response.justification: The candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience as its primary technical qualifier, yet the resume lists no C++ in the skills section and shows only a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments involving C/C++ work alongside a bachelor's thesis project — far short of the minimum. The candidate's 15+ year career is predominantly in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang across web, mobile, and direct mail platforms, with cloud exposure limited to AWS CDK usage and Kubernetes deployment of a personal open-source project. There is no evidence of technical leadership in cloud infrastructure, large-scale distributed systems design, or mentoring engineers in a cloud/DevOps context.
strengths: Demonstrated exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency async I/O)
Strong culture of engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and knowledge sharing through workshops and dojos
Broad polyglot background across embedded, web, mobile, and infrastructure contexts showing adaptability
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments + a thesis project) versus the mandatory 8-year requirement — the single most disqualifying gap
No cloud platform architecture experience at scale; AWS CDK and Kubernetes usage is at the application/deployment level, not infrastructure/compute platform design

No formal technical leadership role: current and past titles are Staff/Senior Software Developer with no evidence of leading teams, setting architectural direction, or owning large ambiguous infrastructure programs
